text: age, in addition to breast-feeding. /c63Do not delay complementary feeding. /c63Feed low-cost home-made complementary foods. /c63Feed complementary food on demand 3-4 times a day. /c63Provide fruits and well cooked vegetables. /c63Observe hygienic practices while preparing and feeding the complementary food. /c63Read nutrition label on baby foods carefully. /c63If breast-feeding fails, the infant needs to be fed animal milk or commercial infant formula. /c63Milk should be boiled before being fed to the baby. /c63To start with, milk may be diluted with an equal volume of water. /c63Full strength milk may be started from 4 weeks of age. /c63Infants fed animal milk should receive supplements of iron and vitamin C. /c63About 120-180 ml of milk should be fed with one teaspoon of sugar per feed,
